name: Synthetic Student Response Validity Conditions Controlled-Comparison Search

search_queries.notes: Used to identify recent multi-model and simulation-fidelity studies.
Used to identify workflow comparisons and response authenticity papers.
Used to identify scoring-adjacent and item-development studies using real-response comparators.
Used to identify model/workflow comparison studies.

search_queries.query: "SMART" "Simulated Students Aligned with Item Response Theory"
"Exploring the Capacity of Large Language Models to Simulate Students' Scientific Thinking"
"Developing Authentic Simulated Learners for Mathematics Teacher Learning"

inclusion_criteria: Primary source with full text or authoritative abstract/metadata available.
Study uses LLM-generated or otherwise synthetic student responses, utterances, or constructed responses.

Study includes at least one of: realism/fidelity metric, model/workflow comparison, prompt/workflow comparison, real-response comparator, human authenticity evaluation, or scoring-adjacent item-difficulty workflow.
Study helps answer whether synthetic student responses provide validity-relevant evidence, or helps bound that claim.
exclusion_criteria: General LLM-in-education papers without generated student responses.
Pure multiple-choice-only simulated response papers unless directly tied to current scorer-validation or item-development boundaries.
Opinion pieces, blog posts, and secondary summaries.
Sources without enough accessible methodological detail for extraction.
dedupe_method: Title, DOI, arXiv identifier, and ACL Anthology identifier were used to deduplicate records; targeted lookups were linked to the same source record when PDF and landing page both existed.

screening_decisions.title: Exploring the Capacity of Large Language Models to Simulate Students' Scientific Thinking: Insights for Responsive Teaching
screening_decisions.reason: Direct multi-model open-ended student-idea realism comparison with explicit metrics and teacher perceptions.

screening_decisions.title: Developing Authentic Simulated Learners for Mathematics Teacher Learning: Insights from Three Approaches with Large Language Models
screening_decisions.reason: Direct comparison of few-shot, fine-tuned, multi-agent, and DPO workflows for student-response authenticity.

screening_decisions.title: SMART: Simulated Students Aligned with Item Response Theory for Question Difficulty Prediction
screening_decisions.reason: Synthetic open-ended responses are used in a scoring-adjacent item difficulty prediction workflow anchored to human-scored real responses and IRT.

screening_decisions.title: Embracing Imperfection: Simulating Students with Diverse Cognitive Levels Using LLM-based Agents
screening_decisions.reason: Constructed-response programming study directly addressing imperfect-response realism, cognitive-level variation, and error plausibility.

screening_decisions.title: Synthetic Students: A Comparative Study of Bug Distribution Between Synthetic and Computing Students
screening_decisions.reason: Highly relevant to bug-distribution realism and prompting effects, but full extraction was deferred pending full-text access verification and line-level extraction.

screening_decisions.title: Can LLMs Effectively Simulate Human Learners? A Study of Teachers' Insights from Tutoring LLM Students
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to stakeholder authenticity judgments, but less direct for automated scoring and model/workflow comparison than included sources.

screening_decisions.title: Do LLMs Make Mistakes Like Students? Exploring Natural Alignments Between Language Models and Human Error Patterns
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to human error-pattern realism, but initial screening suggested a stronger multiple-choice/distractor emphasis and lower fit to constructed-response scorer workflows.

screening_decisions.title: Synthetic Student Responses for Estimating Item Difficulty Parameters
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to synthetic item responses and IRT but outside the current pass's focus on language or constructed-response realism unless a later item-difficulty-specific update is opened.

screening_decisions.title: General LLM tutor or feedback papers without synthetic student response evaluation
screening_decisions.reason: Did not meet the synthetic-response and comparator criteria.

screening_summary: This was a targeted cross-node review update search rather than a new baseline-review question. The included sources were selected because they add direct evidence on realism metrics, model/workflow choice, real-response comparators, and scoring-adjacent item-development use. The strongest scoring-adjacent source is SMART; the other included sources mainly inform conditions for realism and model/workflow claims, not direct scorer validation.
limitations: The pass was targeted rather than exhaustive.
Most included evidence concerns realism/authenticity and item-development conditions, not final validation of operational automated scorers.
Several potentially relevant error-pattern and bug-distribution papers were deferred for deeper full-text extraction.
